etymology.description: 人(人) (rén, "person") + 攴(攴) (pū, "tap, strike") -> symbolizing authority or correction -> dots possibly indicating 水(水) (shuǐ, "water") -> purification ceremony or washing -> final sense related to 修(修) (xiū, "repair") and 滌(滌) (dí, "wash")
etymology.interpretation: The character originally depicts a person warned or corrected by a hand with a stick, symbolizing authority or discipline, linked to 修(修) (xiū, "repair"). However, an alternative interpretation suggests the act of pouring water over a person for purification, aligned with the meaning in 滌(滌) (dí, "wash"). These interpretations explain its association with 'distant' as a reminder of proper conduct or purification.
